title: Diodia radicans
intro: Diodia radicans, also known as Pineland Buttonweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southeastern United States and the Caribbean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Diodia radicans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Diodia radicans is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and creams to take advantage of its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Diodia radicans is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This family is commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The plant is native to the Americas and is often found growing in a variety of habitats. Within the Rubiaceae family, Diodia radicans is part of the tribe Spermaeae. It is a low-growing, spreading plant with small, white or pink flowers.
